snapshot.name: James Carter
snapshot.teaser: 67-year-old male 7 weeks post left MCA infarct with right hemiparesis, gait asymmetry, and reduced community mobility

history.chief_complaint: Weakness and poor balance after stroke
history.hpi: 67-year-old male referred to outpatient PT 7 weeks after a left middle cerebral artery ischemic stroke. Completed acute hospitalization and 2 weeks of inpatient rehab. Main complaints are right leg heaviness, right arm weakness, slow walking, poor endurance, and fear of falling in the community.
history.mechanism_of_injury: Cerebral infarction with residual right hemiparesis

pain.location: Intermittent right shoulder and calf tightness
pain.quality: Stiffness and heaviness rather than sharp pain
pain.aggravating_factors: Longer walking distances, dual-tasking, stairs, fatigue late in the day
pain.easing_factors: Seated rest, pacing, supported stretching, slower task setup

functional_goals: Walk in the grocery store without holding onto cart for support
Climb 12 stairs to church fellowship hall with rail only
Use right hand better during dressing and meal prep

gait.pattern: Hemiparetic gait with decreased right stance time and reduced right foot clearance
gait.observations: Circumduction of right LE during swing, limited right arm swing, slowed turns, mild right knee hyperextension in midstance

eval.notes_seed: Subacute outpatient neuro rehab evaluation after left MCA stroke with right hemiparesis

subjective.chiefComplaint: Right-sided weakness, slow walking, and balance problems after stroke
subjective.historyOfPresentIllness: Patient is 7 weeks post left MCA infarct. Reports improvement since inpatient rehab but still needs a cane outdoors, avoids busy environments, and feels unstable with turning and carrying objects.
subjective.painLocation: Right shoulder and posterior calf tightness intermittently
subjective.painPattern: Worse with fatigue and longer activity bouts
subjective.aggravatingFactors: Community walking, uneven surfaces, stairs, carrying items while walking, multitasking
subjective.easingFactors: Rest breaks, focused pacing, sitting, stretching
subjective.functionalLimitations: Needs cane for community ambulation, step-to stair pattern with rail, requires extra time for dressing, avoids crowded community settings
subjective.priorLevel: Independent household and community mobility without device; drove, attended church weekly, and walked dog daily
subjective.patientGoals: Walk more normally, feel steadier in the community, and improve use of the right side
subjective.redFlags: No recurrent neuro symptoms, chest pain, or new falls since discharge
subjective.additionalHistory: Lives with spouse in two-story home; motivated and compliant with home exercise program

objective.text: Alert, motivated, follows 2-step commands. Mild expressive word-finding delay but communicates needs clearly.

inspection.visual: Mild right UE flexor posturing during gait; slight right trunk lean during stance; fatigues with repeated transfers

palpation.findings: Mild increased tone in right plantarflexors and finger flexors; no tenderness except intermittent right shoulder stiffness

neuro.screening: Right UE/LE coordination impaired, mild right-sided proprioceptive reduction, right LE strength grossly 4-/5 proximally and 3+/5 ankle dorsiflexion, mild spasticity in plantarflexors

functional.assessment: Berg 39/56; TUG 19.4 sec with SBQC; 10MWT self-selected 0.58 m/s; 5xSTS 20.8 sec using left UE support. Requires close supervision for turns and obstacle negotiation.

assessment.primaryImpairments: Residual right hemiparesis, impaired dynamic balance, decreased gait speed, reduced right foot clearance, impaired coordination, and activity-related fatigue after left MCA infarct.
assessment.bodyFunctions: Motor control deficits, spasticity/tone management needs, decreased strength and endurance, impaired postural reactions, reduced single-leg stability on the right.
assessment.activityLimitations: Community ambulation, turning, stairs, carrying objects while walking, prolonged standing, and dressing tasks requiring bilateral coordination.
assessment.participationRestrictions: Limited church participation, grocery shopping, dog walking, and community mobility confidence.
assessment.ptDiagnosis: I69.351 - Hemiplegia and hemiparesis following cerebral infarction affecting right dominant side; I69.398 - Other sequelae of cerebral infarction.
assessment.prognosticFactors: Positive family support, recent onset, strong motivation, prior inpatient rehab gains; complexity increased by diabetes and persistent gait/balance deficits.
assessment.clinicalReasoning: Presentation is consistent with subacute post-stroke hemiparesis with community mobility limitations. Skilled PT is indicated for high-repetition task-specific gait training, balance retraining, transfer/stair practice, and progressive conditioning to reduce fall risk and improve participation.

plan.treatmentPlan: Task-specific gait and balance training, obstacle/turning practice, transfer and stair work, progressive LE strengthening, reactive balance, dual-task progression, caregiver education, and brace management follow-up as needed.
plan.patientEducation: Fall risk reduction, home safety, energy conservation, stroke recovery expectations, daily walking dosage, and pacing strategies for fatigue.

goal_1.goalText: Improve self-selected gait speed from 0.58 m/s to at least 0.8 m/s within 8 weeks for better limited community ambulation.

goal_2.goalText: Improve Berg Balance Scale from 39/56 to at least 46/56 within 6-8 weeks to reduce fall risk.

goal_3.goalText: Ascend and descend 12 stairs with one rail and step-through pattern under supervision within 8 weeks.

goal_4.goalText: Ambulate 300 feet with least restrictive device and no loss of balance during turns within 8 weeks.

ex_1.exerciseText: Massed sit-to-stand practice from standard chair with symmetrical loading cueing - 3x8, daily.

ex_2.exerciseText: Supported right LE step taps and step-ups for foot clearance and limb advancement - 3x10, 5x/week.

ex_3.exerciseText: Overground gait intervals with cane emphasizing right foot clearance, stance time, and arm swing - 10-15 minutes, daily.

ex_4.exerciseText: Standing weight shift and reaching tasks toward right side for balance confidence - 3x2 minutes, daily.

diagnosisCodes.description: Hemiplegia and hemiparesis following cerebral infarction affecting right dominant side
diagnosisCodes.label: I69.351 - Hemiplegia and hemiparesis following cerebral infarction affecting right dominant side
I69.398 - Other sequelae of cerebral infarction

ordersReferrals.details: Coordinate with OT for upper-extremity motor recovery and ADL carryover.
Continue AFO and review fit if foot clearance or skin issues change.
